pick.rationale: TCU holds a significant analytical edge with a KenPom rank of 43 and strong defensive efficiency (rank 30), while Ohio State's ratings are unavailable suggesting they are a weaker team. The Horned Frogs' experience in the competitive Big 12 and their balanced profile give them an advantage over an Ohio State team that lacks comparable metrics. Though 8/9 matchups are typically close, TCU's superior defensive capabilities should be the deciding factor.

The game features two teams from strong conferences that have shown both impressive wins and unexpected losses during the 2025–26 season. Ohio State enters the tournament as the No. 8 seed, while TCU comes in as the No. 9 seed. Because of how evenly matched the teams are, this contest is expected to be one of the closest games of the first round. Both teams have different playing styles. Ohio State relies on efficient shooting and scoring from its backcourt, while TCU depends on physical defense and forcing turnovers to create scoring opportunities. Match Details Match TCU vs Ohio State Tournament NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament 2026 Round Round of 64 Date Thursday, March 19, 2026 Time 12:15 PM ET Venue Bon Secours Wellness Arena, Greenville, SC TV Channel CBS Team Records and Tournament Context Team Record Conference TCU Horned Frogs 22–11 Big 12 Ohio State Buckeyes 21–12 Big Ten Ohio State returns to the NCAA Tournament for the first time since 2022. The Buckeyes had an inconsistent regular season but improved late in the year to secure their place in March Madness. TCU also battled through a competitive conference schedule. The Horned Frogs finished sixth in the Big 12, widely considered one of the toughest leagues in college basketball. Their schedule included several high-level opponents, helping prepare them for the intensity of tournament play. Ohio State is a slight favorite, but the spread remains small, reflecting the competitive balance between the two teams. result.snippet: The 2025-26 Ohio State Buckeyes men's basketball team represents the Ohio State University during the 2025-26 NCAA Division I men's basketball season. They are led by second-year head coach Jake Diebler, and play their home games at Value City Arena in Columbus, Ohio , as a member of the Big Ten Conference.
